title: BEM CSS Selector and Layout Contract
purpose: Define a compact, testable contract for the CSS rules used by the BEM methodology.
scope: This specification covers class-based selectors, nested selector limits, mixes, and the layout rules that keep BEM blocks reusable. It does not define class-name syntax or build tooling.
context: The official BEM documentation presents BEM as a reusable component methodology with class-based selector rules and mix-based composition. This example translates those rules into requirement form so the repository can show a real, useful specification instead of a synthetic one.

requirements.title: Use class selectors for BEM entities
requirements.statement: BEM entity styling MUST use class selectors.

requirements.title: Avoid tag and ID selectors
requirements.statement: BEM entity styling MUST NOT use tag or ID selectors.

requirements.title: Avoid tag-and-class selectors
requirements.statement: BEM entity styling SHOULD NOT combine tags and classes in a selector.

requirements.title: Limit nested selectors to block state or theme set changes
requirements.statement: Nested selectors MAY change element styles relative to a block state or theme set.

requirements.title: Avoid combined selectors
requirements.statement: Combined selectors SHOULD NOT be used.

requirements.title: Allow mixes to combine entities on one node
requirements.statement: A mix MAY combine multiple BEM entities on the same DOM node.

requirements.title: Use mixes for external geometry and positioning
requirements.statement: External geometry and positioning SHOULD be set via the parent block or a mix.

requirements.title: Avoid wrapper-heavy positioning
requirements.statement: Implementations SHOULD use mixes instead of wrapper blocks when positioning one block relative to another.

requirements.title: Use selector names that accurately describe the entity
requirements.statement: The name of a selector SHOULD fully and accurately describe the BEM entity it represents.
notes: Example class names from the official docs include `menu__item`, `menu_hidden`, `menu__item_visible`, and `button button_size_m`.
The example stays with the classic naming scheme and does not cover alternative naming variants.
